key_insight: The paper learns a Riemannian metric directly from data by matching the carré du champ operator via denoising, enabling geometry estimation without explicit graphs or kernels.
engineering_relevance: This provides a scalable, graph-free way to recover data geometry for downstream learning and analysis, with amortized inference that is much faster than k-NN diffusion methods.
method: Train a neural network with random perturbations of samples to model the carré du champ as a conditional expectation.
Use diffusion geometry to translate the learned operator into Riemannian geometric quantities.
Avoid explicit kernel construction and nearest-neighbor graph building at inference time.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that the method matches or improves k-NN diffusion geometry estimators while enabling amortized inference up to 400× faster, including on high-dimensional image data.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality of the denoising perturbation model and may be sensitive when nearest-neighbor geometry is already unreliable or the manifold structure is weakly sampled.
paper_title: Riemannian Metric Matching for Scalable Geometric Modeling of Distributions
